title: Maharashtrian Vada Pav

subs.name: equal weight boiled raw banana (kachche kela) mashed with the same spicing — a popular variation

ingredients.name: oil, for tempering + deep-frying
visual_checks: 500 ml for deep-frying; 1 tsp extra for potato tempering
ingredients.notes: 500 ml for deep-frying; 1 tsp extra for potato tempering

ingredients.name: rice flour (for extra crispiness in batter)

subs.name: omit — the batter works without it, just slightly less crispy
any soft dinner roll or milk bun; a pav bun bought from a bakery is ideal

steps.text: Heat 1 tsp oil in a kadai on medium. Add mustard seeds; once they pop add asafoetida, curry leaves, garlic and green chilli and fry for 30 seconds. Add turmeric and immediately tip in the mashed potato. Mix well, add lemon juice, salt and coriander leaves. Cool the filling to room temperature before shaping.

Divide the potato filling into 8 equal portions and roll each into a smooth ball, then flatten slightly into a disc about 6 cm wide and 2 cm thick. Place on a tray and refrigerate for 10 minutes — cold filling holds its shape better during frying.

Make the batter: whisk together besan, rice flour, red chilli powder, a pinch of turmeric, baking soda and salt. Add water gradually, whisking until the batter is smooth and coats the back of a spoon — thick enough to cling to the vada without dripping off immediately.

Heat oil in a kadai to 175°C (a small drop of batter should surface within 2 seconds). Dip each potato disc into the batter, letting the excess drip off, and gently slide it into the hot oil. Fry 2–3 vadas at a time for 3–4 minutes, turning occasionally, until the crust is golden and blistered all over. Drain on paper.

Slit the pav rolls horizontally. Smear the cut surface of one side with green coriander chutney and the other with dry garlic-coconut chutney (see notes). Place a hot vada inside, press gently so the chutney grips it, and serve immediately while the crust is still crackling.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: potato → equal weight boiled raw banana (kachche kela) mashed with the same spicing — a popular variation; bread → any soft dinner roll or milk bun; a pav bun bought from a bakery is ideal; rice-flour → omit — the batter works without it, just slightly less crispy
